images.alt:
Base fabric: plain weave

Size style: Naga-haori

Family crests (mon) present: none

Dimensions: 46 inches (117 cm) from sleeve-end to sleeve-end and 37 inches (94 cm) in height

This composition is characterized by a distinctive pattern achieved entirely through the intricate shibori (resist dyeing) technique. The design features repeating yabane motifs rendered in a rich russet against a golden-yellow ground. Even the seemingly simple background demonstrates the shibori technique, exhibiting a subtle textural pattern that adds depth and dimension to the surface.  The design's abstraction of natural forms into geometric patterns reflects the Japanese artistic principle of simplification and stylization, which has been present in Japanese textile arts since the Edo period. The diagonal placement of the motifs creates a dynamic sense of movement across the garment, while the consistent spacing maintains visual harmony - a principle known as "ma" in Japanese aesthetics. The color combination of warm gold and deep red is characteristic of traditional Japanese color sensibilities, particularly those seen in kosode (predecessor to the kimono) from the 17th and 18th centuries. What's particularly interesting about this piece is how it demonstrates the sophisticated understanding of repeating patterns and geometric abstraction that Japanese textile artists had developed long before similar aesthetic approaches emerged in Western decorative arts. The overall effect, achieved solely through the masterful manipulation of the shibori technique, creates a bold, graphic quality that feels remarkably modern despite its traditional techniques and likely historical origins. The haori shows how Japanese textile artists achieved a perfect balance between decorative impact and elegant restraint, a principle that would later become central to modernist design philosophy in the West.

images.alt: A highly detailed handcrafted plasticine puppet of a slender black cat, designed in a dark gothic claymation style. The cat features visible sculpting textures, subtle thumbprints, and delicate tool marks on its matte black clay body. It has large, expressive glowing amber eyes that look like polished glass beads and long, wiry whiskers. The cat is perched on a crooked, mossy stone ledge under the pale, eerie light of a silver moon. The atmosphere is mysterious and whimsical, with a slight cinematic fog swirling at the base. High-contrast lighting creates dramatic shadows, with cool blue rim lighting highlighting the puppet's silhouette against a backdrop of gnarled, leafless trees and a silhouetted Victorian manor. The composition captures the charm of a physical miniature set, emphasizing the tactile textures of modeling clay, wire armatures, and weathered wood. 8k resolution, stop-motion animation aesthetic, moody and atmospheric

images.alt: Fairytale passenger princess in the front seat of a 1990s Toyota Camry at a Sonic drive-in at night, wearing a sparkling pale-blue ball gown and jeweled tiara, laughing excitedly at the camera. Holding a large Sonic fountain drink and an oversized double cheeseburger partially unwrapped with melted cheese, lettuce, and tomato. Sonic bags and wrappers scattered around the interior, glowing Sonic signage outside the windows. Direct on-camera flash, candid snapshot feel, authentic 90s Camry interior, shallow depth of field, realistic skin and detailed food, cinematic nighttime mood. Strong Polaroid aesthetic with vintage instant-film colors, warm nostalgic tones, soft bloom, film grain, dust specks, slight vignette, imperfect exposure, thick white Polaroid border, square composition, ultra-realistic documentary-style photography, nostalgic Americana, fairy-tale elegance meets fast-food indulgence.

You want to take The KittenBot on a walk?

Simply reply to this post and advise a city.

Then The KittenBot will dream about this city! #AIGenerated

See example below.
NB: Only followers can reply. Up to you to join the fun!

images.alt:
On Mondays and Sundays, a story will be told by The KittenBot.

The KittenBot checks the last reply of this post in order to know in which city the story should take place.


In case you want to vote for a city:

+ Please subscribe to this account (i.e. follow), and

+ Then, on the Monday of your choice (or the Sunday of your choice), before 13:00 (UTC), Please reply to this post: your reply should contain the name of the city you chose.


Example:
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "Paris,France".

Other examples:
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "Paris,FR".
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "London,GB".
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "London,UK".
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "London,Ontario,CA".
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "London,CA".
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "London,US".
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "London,Kentucky,US".
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "Milan,IT".


As shown in the example, the reply should mention me.

Please note that they are no space character before the colon character.
The whole reply should be identical as the example: only the text between the first double quote and the second double quote can be changed.


Please note that your reply must be sent:
+ after that The RandomBot chose a city.
+ before that The KittenBot starts verifying the chosen city.

The KittenBot always considers that the last reply embeds the name of the chosen city.

Indeed:

+ At 13:00 (UTC), The KittenBot checks the last post of this thread in order to know the city that was chosen. The KittenBot then tells a story that could happen in this city.

+ At 13:05 (UTC), The RandomBot randomly chooses another city. This city will be taken into account during the next run of The KittenBot if no other reply is posted.



In case you are happy with the story written by the bot, please do not hesitate to:

+ like this post, and

+ repost this post, and

+ like the reply containing the story, and

+ repost the reply containing the story.

The KittenBot answers directly to posts without the intervention of UVV!

You just have to quote The KittenBot in a post.

Example:
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "...".

The KittenBot sleeps very often, and also plays with coton balls... except at 13:00 (GMT,UTC), on Mondays and Saturdays.

Indeed, at this exact time on Mondays and Saturdays, The KittenBot shares as much joy and happiness as possible with who has a query!

The KittenBot tells a few actions that The KittenBot would likely attempt if The KittenBot was in the city you chose. You just have to put the name of the city of your choice between quotes in the post that you send.

Indeed, since May 31st, the interactive bot is now released!
Up to you to play! Will you dare?  :-)

Example:
@kittenuvv.bsky.social: I choose "Paris,FR".

Technical details: the stories created by The KittenBot tend to be as creative as possible, yet other type of activities are restricted on purpose in order to avoid cases when a user would intently requests strange actions. Only a city name (and a country code) are expected, therefore do not expect that The KittenBot will tell anything except a story suitable for all audiences. Most likely, the story will sound like a fairy tale... with or without fairies, and eventually with a robotic pet...

In order to trigger as much originality as possible, The KittenBot checks current weather at the city you choose. The KittenBot sometimes takes also into account the current local time of this city. The KittenBot uses the list of well known monuments and places of the city you choose as inspiration.
The Weather data is provided by OpenWeather© (https://OpenWeatherMap.org/).

record.bridgyOriginalText:
AI prompting isn't rocket science. Blind trust is the real danger.

People think prompt engineering is only for tech experts. They assume you need to be a coder or data scientist to get good results. They think you can just trust the output.

Prompting is just clear communication. You already do this in emails and meetings. The real danger is assuming AI is always right. It is not. It will confidently give you wrong answers or made up facts. That is not a bug. It is how it works. (1/3)
